queue.title: Video sync: add playPaired(fg, bg) + waitCanPlay() helpers; replace all independent fg/bg play() calls
queue.note: Call sites: applyActive() and setDialState(ACTIVE). Both currently play fg and bg independently.

queue.title: Video sync: pause videos when not visible — IDLE pauses fgVideo+bgVideo; ACTIVE pauses genericVideo; tab hidden pauses all
queue.note: setDialState(IDLE) must pause fgVideo + bgVideo. setDialState(ACTIVE) must pause genericVideo. onVis (tab hidden) must pause all active videos and resume on return. Pool videos (poolPrev/poolNext) are exempt — they play off-screen to stay buffered. Also pause outgoing visibleVideo when demoted to pool slot after a swap.

queue.title: Video sync: dedicated RAF drift monitor — pause ahead video if |fg - bg| > threshold; only runs in ACTIVE/ENGAGED
queue.note: Replace syncFgToBg() in draw() with a separate RAF loop scoped to dialState !== IDLE. Threshold: 0.1s desktop / 0.3s mobile. Use intentionallyPaused flag to avoid fighting tab-hidden or deliberate pauses. Cancel in cleanup/destroy.

queue.title: Video sync: GSAP 0.2s crossfade on project switch in applyActive() (skip if prefers-reduced-motion)
queue.note: No crossfade on IDLE↔ACTIVE transitions — genericVideo/bgVideo opacity changes are already instant and behind the dial.

queue.title: Video sync: QA adjacent switch, non-adjacent switch, IDLE↔ACTIVE transitions, tab hidden/return, Barba return, mobile sync
queue.note: Blocked on video-sync-rearchitect. Current src-swap + pool approach still produces 0.3s load lag on every switch (including repeat adjacent switches that should be instant). Root cause: bg video always reloads on src change; pool only helps fg. Architecture must change before QA is useful.

queue.title: Video sync: re-arch to parallel video elements — N fg + N bg elements loaded simultaneously, opacity switching
queue.note: Current approach (2 pool + 1 visible fg, 1 bg) is fundamentally flawed: bg.src must change on every switch → browser reload → load lag. Even with playPaired gen-counter fix, the bg reload is unavoidable. New approach: on init, create N fg + N bg <video> elements (one per project, max 8). All load in parallel with preload=auto. Active pair: opacity 1. All others: opacity 0. No src changes ever — switching is purely opacity. Crossfade: gsap.to(outgoing, {opacity:0}) + gsap.to(incoming, {opacity:1}). Drift monitor: same as current. Barba cleanup: pause + remove all N*2 elements. Cost: 8 simultaneous loads vs 3, but all are small loop videos and loads are staggered by browser.
queue.blockedOn: design sign-off — confirm acceptable to load all N videos on init
